scope: ACL 2027 supplement InternNav upload-gate closure-card visual-density iteration

motivation.target_page_before: The official-scene submission closure table page was the sparsest non-reference page after earlier supplement iterations.
motivation.goal_alignment: Replace a sparse status table page with a render-heavy visual card while preserving machine-readable closure artifacts and an explicit AI-slot claim boundary.

slot_composer.critical_text_policy: Critical labels, closure gates, claim-boundary text, and paper caption are drawn by deterministic code or LaTeX. The AI slot is a small upload-approval schematic.

local_visual_review.notes: Standalone figure and rendered PDF page show no clipping or caption collision. The large media panel uses registered navigation stills and route overlays. The AI upload-gate schematic is visually separate and disclosed. Remaining risk: some in-figure gate text is small at paper scale.

verification.targeted_red: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "internnav_has_upload_gate_closure_card or internnav_upload_gate_closure_card" failed before implementation because the figure reference and registry entry were absent.
verification.targeted_green: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "internnav_has_upload_gate_closure_card or internnav_upload_gate_closure_card" passed: 2 passed, 54 deselected.
verification.build: make -C paper acl27-supplement passed and produced a 44-page A4 supplement.
verification.pdf_text_scan: pdftotext found the Figure S25 caption and AI-generated upload-gate schematic disclosure; the old Official-scene submission closure status table caption was absent. A separate risk-token scan found no old red-material caption tokens, fig_vlm_grounding_cases, author-identifying tokens, or local path tokens.

claim_boundary.paper_use: visual closure-card replacement for a sparse official-scene submission status table in the InternNav supplement section
claim_boundary.notes: The AI upload-gate slot is not navigation evidence. Evidence-bearing panels are registered navigation stills and route overlays; quantitative claims remain tied to the 99-episode paired run and official-scene closure artifacts.
